records.ety: [L. barbarismus, Gr. barbarismo`s; cf. F. barbarisme.]

defs: An uncivilized state or condition; rudeness of manners; ignorance of arts, learning, and literature; barbarousness.
A barbarous, cruel, or brutal action; an outrage.
quotes: A heinous barbarism . . . against the honor of marriage.

defs: An offense against purity of style or language; any form of speech contrary to the pure idioms of a particular language. See Solecism.
quotes: The Greeks were the first that branded a foreign term in any of their writers with the odious name of barbarism.
